task_completion.description: Navigate the map to the intersection of Madrid's latitude and Paris's longitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view should be centered approximately at 40.4°N, 2.3°E, which is in the Balearic Sea (Mediterranean) off the eastern coast of Spain.
The 'field_observations' layer should be highlighted or selected in the layer panel, indicating it is the active layer for editing.

task_completion.description: Create a new point feature at the intersection location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new point feature should be added or in the process of being added (e.g., crosshairs active, digitizing mode on) at the correct map location.

The text 'Distress Signal' should be visible in the attribute entry form for the new point. (Note: Since the exact 'name' column is missing from the schema, accept this text entered into any available string field such as 'observer_name' or 'location').

task_completion.description: Set the Notes to 'Intersection of Madrid and Paris'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Intersection of Madrid and Paris' should be visible in the attribute entry form. (Note: Accept this text entered into any available field due to the missing 'notes' column).

integrity.description: Agent used the GIS application GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the QField map interface, layer panels, and attribute forms, rather than terminal windows or SQLite command prompts.

integrity.description: Attributes were entered using the application's native feature form.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text entry for 'Distress Signal' and the notes should appear inside the QField feature attribute dialog, not in a separate text editor or script.

integrity.description: The point was created visually on the map.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use the map crosshairs or digitizing tools to place the point, rather than executing a script to inject coordinates directly into the database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target layer: 'field_observations'. Target feature name: 'Distress Signal'. Target notes: 'Intersection of Madrid and Paris'. Note: The database is missing 'Madrid' and the 'field_observations' layer lacks 'name' and 'notes' columns. The agent may use external knowledge to find Madrid's latitude (~40.4°N) and Paris's longitude (~2.3°E) and may enter the requested text into alternative fields (like 'observer_name' or 'location'). The intersection is approximately at 40.4°N, 2.3°E (in the Balearic Sea east of Spain).
